title: The One With The Boob Job

lines.text: Chandler and Monica's apartment. Chandler and Monica are looking through some papers.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Did you see our bank statement? Can this be right?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know...God. I haven't seen my savings take a hit like this since I was a kid and they came up with double-stuffed Oreos. What happened to all our money?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm not sure what they did, but I'm inclined to blame Enron.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I guess with you doing the internship, we're just spending more than we're bringing in.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Maybe I should quit and get a job that pays.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, But you're finally doing something that you love! I can't ask you to give that up. Though it'd be nice if the thing that you love was y'know... finding gold.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You know what? You know what? I think we're making too big a deal out of this. ok? So we pay our bills a little late this month and maybe next month we cut back on a few things. And maybe we start eating out of Joey's refrigerator for a change. You're chef... what can you make out of backing soda and beer?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ok worse case scenario is...we borrow some money from my parents.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No! We're not borrowing money.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Because we don't do that. We are Bings! And if there's one thing my father taught me was... well to always knock before going into the pool house... but the other thing was never borrow money.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wow! I Had no idea you had this much pride.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's right! I do! And I'm your man. And I'm going to get us through this situation even if it means you working twice as hard.
Opening Credits
Phoebe's apartment. Mike and Phoebe seem to have finished watching a movie on television.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: I'm gonna go. (He kisses Phoebe on the cheek)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Why??

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: I haven't been home in a couple of days and I need to get some more clothes.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh...you don't have to go, I have something that will fit you.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: I put that tube top on as a joke.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I want you to stay.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: I want to stay too but I've gone as bad as much use out of these boxers as I can..

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Why don't you turn them inside ou...

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Done it. (Phoebe becomes a little more subdued) I'll be back in a couple of hours.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'll miss you.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Me too. (He leaves. A few moments later, he enters again.) You know what? I just realised something. I don't wanna go home.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Great! Ok...I'll go get the tube top.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: No, no! What I mean is, I hate going back to my apartment now... and partly because I live above a known crack den but... mostly because when I'm there, It's just, I really miss you. So.. do you want to move in together?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wow, Mike Hannigan...You sure know how to make a girl say "Hell yeah!"

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: So we're doing it?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah! Let's do it! Let's live together! (They embrace and Mike kisses Phoebe) Oh god, we're really going to move in together!

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I've always wanted to live with a guy. "Pick up your socks!" "Put down the toilet seat!" "No! We're not having sex anymore!" It's gonna be fun!
Joey's apartment. Monica enters.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey Joey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Listen...I need to know that what I'm about to ask you, will never get back to Chandler.
Joey frowns...then looks as if he understands

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I'd be lying if I said I haven't thought about it myself. Chandler is my best friend, it would be wrong. Good...(He winks)...But wrong.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (Looks exasperated) Ok first of all...It would be great. But that's not what I'm here to talk to you about. I need to borrow some money.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Aww, I don't know Monica y'know... erm... lending friends money is always a mistake.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: But Chandler lent you money!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: And I think he would tell you it was a mistake.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Come on...I just need it for some rent and..and some other bills.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh...how much?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Two thousand dollars?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Two thousand dollars!? What do you think I am? I soap opera star!?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That's right I am! (Opens drawer and rummages through it. Rachel enters)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi you guys!

lines.speaker: Joey & Monica
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oo...what's in the bag?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh er... well you know Emma started crawling? I realised that this place, is very unsafe for a baby. So I went to the store and got some stuff to baby-proof the apartment.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh...baby-proofing... Why is this such a big deal now? Y'know, when I was a kid it was like.. "Whoops! Joey fell down the stairs!" or er.. "Whoops! Joey electrocuted himself again!" Huh!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Anyway erm, are you going to get a handyman to install this stuff?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No. I was just going to do this myself. (Joey makes a sarcastic laugh.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You're gonna do it?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, Why? You don't think a woman can do this?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, women can. You can't.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Monica...would you please tell Joey that he is a pig?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You're a pig. And you can't do this.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wha!? What!? Come on! I found the hardware store all by myself!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: The hardware store is right down the street.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: There is a hardware store right down the street?
Central Perk. Ross, Chandler and Monica are sitting on the couch. Phoebe and Mike enters.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey you guys! Ok, we've got great news.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Phoebe and I are moving in together.

lines.speaker: Monica, Chandler, Ross
lines.text: Congratulations!/Good for you!/Great!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I know it's so exciting! You know I've never lived with a guy before.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well you know it's just like living with a girl. Only they don't steal your makeup. Unless they're playing "This is what my sister would look like" (Looks at Chandler)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah.... she's not so cute.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: I'm gonna go to the bathroom.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, well you put down the toilet seat.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Yes dear.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Is that a bit you guys do?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uh huh...we're playing you two.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: We don't do that! Tell her we don't do that!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes dear.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I can't believe you guys are moving in together. That's, that's great! I mean...I'm happy for you guys..

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I hear wedding bells.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Monica slow down! Ok? I'm just excited to be living with him. You know I mean, I don't know, Can I see someday being married to Mike? Sure! Yeah. Y'know..I can picture myself walking down the aisle in a wedding dress that highlights my breasts in an obvious yet classy way. But do I want that house in Connecticut...you know..near the good schools where Mike and I can send Sophie and Mike Junior.. Oh my god I do.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Phoebe, I had no idea you were so conventional.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I know! I guess I am! Oh my god! Load up the Volvo I want to be a soccer mom!

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: You ready to go?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah! You bet roomy!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Don't you mean..groomy?

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: What are you talking about?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh please, these guys, we haven't even moved in yet and they have us picking out china patterns. (Mike seems to gag a little...and laughs nervously. They begin to leave. Phoebe bolts back)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: China patterns!!!!!
Joey's apartment. Rachel is fiddling with the drawer.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: This is easy...Can't do this! (Moves away...and finds her sweater sleeve stuck to the drawer) Oh! Wow! Seriously I can't do this. (Fiddles more)
Central Perk. Chandler sits on the couch. Joey sits at the round table

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey Joe!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Listen er..I need to ask you a favor but you can't tell Monica anything about it.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I thought you didn't have secrets from Monica.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And that would have made the official party line. (Joey nods) Monica and I are having a little financial trouble.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, I know.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What? What do you mean you know?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Err... I just figured it out! You know, I mean you're not working and the economy is bad.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh! Right.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (to himself) That's the fastest I have ever thought!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Anyway, err... I need to borrow some money.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh! Sure! How much? Two thousand dollars?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes! Two thousand dollars exactly! How do you know that! (Joey begins writing a cheque)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Err...Well I...Know how much you used to make and I know how much your rent is. (shrugs)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh ok.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (To himself) I am on fire!
Chandler (Stands up and walks to Joey): Listen...this is really nice. Do you... (sees his chequebook) Did you write a cheque to Monica for two thousand dollars? Did Monica borrow money from you?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Err... Kind of.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I can't believe her! Did she tell you we were having money problems?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh no no no no no... It wasn't... It wasn't because of your money problems, it was for something for her.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Something personal.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What would she get for herself for two thousand dollars that she wouldn't tell me about?
Woman with huge breasts cuts across them. Takes Joey's coffee

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Excuse me.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Boob job.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I don't want her to get a boob job! That's crazy!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well it's...It's not that crazy okay? Making them smaller, that would be crazy.
Phoebe's apartment. Phoebe and Mike are packing stuff.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Well hey, I wanna ask you about Monica's little "groomy" joke.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh! Alright. Well I think the reason people laugh is becuase it's a play on the word roomy.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: I get the joke. Sophisticated as it was. Now the thing I wanna say is... maybe we should have talked about this before. Us living together, you're not expecting a proposal, right?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh no! No no! Not at all. We're just moving in right now. See where it goes.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Yeah well, that's the thing. For me it's as far as it can ever go.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Look. Phoebe, I-I love you. Very much. But I never want to get married again.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh. Wow.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: It's just my first marriage was, you know such a disaster. I kind of lost faith in the whole idea.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Was it really that bad?

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: At one point near the end she deliberately defecated.....

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ok, well that's bad. But don't you think it might be different with someone else? Perhaps a blonde who always uses a toilet. Except for once in the ocean.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Look it's not about who I would marry. And I was certain the first time I got married it would last forever. And I was totally wrong!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: But it's just...

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Look Phoebe, It's not about you. I just never wanna get married again.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh!

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: I'm sorry. Are you ok with that? Cause if not...maybe us moving in together isn't the best idea.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No! I definitely don't wanna get married. No I just wanted to make sure you didn't want to too. Whew! Coz you know when we move in and you start changing your mind there's gonna be hell to pay mister!

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Trust me, I will never...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah I get that.
Joey's apartment. Rachel is flipping through magazines. Handyman is installing things. Monica enters.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So you gave in and decided to call someone?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, I don't know who I was kidding. I can barely use chopsticks.

lines.speaker: Handyman
lines.text: You're all set. (picks up his bag and starts to leave)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh thank you so much. (Picks up the guy's spirit level) Oh oh wait! You forgot your erm...Your game. (hands it to him)

lines.speaker: Handyman
lines.text: Thanks.....
Chandler enters

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey Rach! There she is...My perfectly proportioned wife.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Don't look at me I never get his jokes.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Thank you?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh no..don't thank me. Thank you. You know there's not one thing I would change about you? Not one single thing! And definitely not... two... single things.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ok, you're being wierd. Do you want sex or did you do something bad?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No no! I just love the way you look, I am warm, for your form.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ok..You know the old classics you know,You look nice? They're still ok.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well yes yes... You look nice can mean that your face looks nice. I want to compliment your body. I mean..I wouldn't change it. At all. And more specifically, I wouldn't want anything to get any bigger.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I mean, You wouldn't want any part of me to get any bigger would you? Don't answer that.
He leaves

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Just when you thought that dude couldn't get any wierder.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know why do you think he was so worried about me getting bigger?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don't know! I mean, what brought that on?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my god. We're trying to get pregnant so he's probably starting to freak out about the fact that my body is going to change.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No you really think that's what it is?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You heard him! "No bigger!" "You're perfect!" "Just don't get any bigger!" Oh my god he sounded just like my high school wrestling coach. You know what? I'm going to have to talk to Chandler.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah! If you don't I will! Of course your body's gonna change. Your breasts are gonna get bigger, your ass is gonna get bigger, you're gonna lose bladder control. (she starts sobbing) God! It's just such a magical time!
Ross's apartment. Phoebe is at the door.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hi

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Listen, I wanted to ask you something about marriage.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh great now you're seeking me out to make jokes? I mean I can see for all hanging out but to come to my home!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No...I really wanted to know how you feel about it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Mike doesn't ever wanna get married.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Never?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Never.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: wow, are you still going to move in with him?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I want to, but I just want you to tell me that marriage isn't really that big a deal. You know that I won't, I won't be missing out on anything. That marriage stinks!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, marriage... stinks! I mean if you wanna see a man gain weight and a woman stop shaving? Get them married.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That's not how you really feel is it?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, I'm sorry. Look I don't think that's what you wanna hear right now but I can't help it. I love marriage.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Seriously? You divorce-o?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: If you have to call me name, I prefer "Ross the Divorcer". It's just cooler. Look, I know my marriage isn't exactly work out. But I love to be that committed to another person. And Carol had some good times before she became a lesbian... and once afterward. I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It's ok that's how you feel.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: But come on! I mean living together will be great! I mean you guys have so much fun and you love Mike.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I do love Mike.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah see? And you are so excited about moving in together before, and you know what? You should be. It's a big deal!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah I guess you're right. Yeah thanks. This helped. Thanks.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: The Divorcer, to the rescue!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It's not cooler.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah I just hurt it.
Joey's apartment. Joey enters carrying a tub of ice cream. He sets it on the table, takes off his jacket and struggles with the drawer. It cannot be opened.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Rach?! (He walks over to the fridge to put it in, but it cannot be opened either) Rachel!!!!? (He walks to the bathroom and tries to lift the toilet seat cover. It won't come up.) So I can't do anything I like????
Chandler and Monica's apartment. Chandler enters. Rachel and monica are seated at the table.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey Rach! Ah........ Perfection. (kisses monica) Wouldn't change a thing. Not a thing.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Honey?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: About that? Erm...I'm going to change.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: But honey you don't have to.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'm going to get bigger!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Honey I... I love your breasts the way they are!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Argh. fascist

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, my breasts are going to get bigger weather you like it or not! And you know what? It's not just my breasts. My ass is going to get bigger too.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: your ass?!?!?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Man, don't be surprised if her hands and her feet get bigger too!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: They...do that?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It's kind of a package deal!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: God why why would you want to do that to yourself!?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I thought I was something that we both wanted!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Alright look, if it means that much to you, a may be able to get on more with the big boobs. But the giant ass and the big clown feet?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my god Chandler! If you can't handle this, what are you going to be like in the hospital? With the blood and the screaming and the little present that's shooting out of her!?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (sedated) What?
Joey's apartment. He's prying open the drawer with a crowbar to no avail. Monica, Rachel and Chandler enter.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey! Why did you tell Chandler that Monica was getting a boob job?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Because she is!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Joey, Chandler knows I borrowed the money.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Mmmm hmm! For your boob job!

lines.speaker: Monica, Rachel, Chandler
lines.text: It's over/Joe!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: OK so I'm out four thousand dollars and nobody's boobs are getting any bigger?
Phoebe's apartment. Ross is helping with the packing, Phoebe and Mike are also there.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey! What do you guys think about this. "Ross: The Divorce-Force".

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Better.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Very cool.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey Pheebs, you know I'm i'm really glad you came to talk to me the other day and I hope I was a little helpful.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh yeah you were helpful! Yeah, no, thanks you.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Good, good. Yeah coz the more I thought about it, the more I realised I don't think marriage is neccessarily the right path for you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, I know the other day in the coffeehouse you were caught up in the whole soccer mom thing? but is that really you? I mean can you honestly picture yourself in a Volvo?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: They are awfully boxy...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I don't know you'd be so bored with marriage. I mean it's so... normal.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uh huh.
Chandler, Monica, Joey and Rachel are carrying a couch through the door

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey hey, can I help?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well! We er..climbed up four flights of stairs, manueveored a narrow hallway, dodged a rabid pitbul... but these last three feet are where it gets really tricky.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You know sometimes your words... they hurt.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey uh, where do you guys want this?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, seriously coz this is really heavy. (She suddenly lets go) I mean not for me because i'm only pretending to hold this, but for these guys.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Just one last time erm... the marriage thing... there's no wiggle room? None at all?

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: No but... You don't want to get married either right?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Right. Except that I do want to get married.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (voice strained) Couldn't have this conversation down at the truck huh?

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: You wanna get married?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Someday.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Aaaaand....... hernia.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I haven't exactly had a normal life and I never really felt I was missing out on anything but it just feels that now it's my turn some of the regular stuff.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: But if you wanna get married why didn't you say something before?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Because I just didn't know how much I wanted it. And I love you, and I wanted to live with you.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: I want to live with you too! Let's do that!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: But I don't think I can! it was ok to move in when I didn't know what was gonna happen, but I can't move in knowing that nothing is ever gonna happen.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Can we at least try living together? I mean you might change your mind about marriage.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Are you gonna change yours?

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Me neither. I think I need to be with someone who wants what I want.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: But I don't want this to end.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I don't want it to end either.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: I can't believe this is gonna end. I guess I'll have my stuff packed up.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Ok... so...(They hug) Goodbye.

lines.speaker: Chandler/Joey/Rachel/Monica
lines.text: Bye bye Mike!/Cya mike!/Bye mike!/Bye bye now!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm so sorry Pheebs.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: We're all sorry... (They have a group hug)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ah, look on the bright side, I mean you won't have to live with this ugly chair! That was here already huh? I love you. (they hug again)
Ending Credits
Joey's apartment. Joey is struggling with the fridge. He finally gets it open and falls on the floor

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: AH HAH! I DID IT! HA HA! Alright... (He takes a box out, about to close fridge, then thinks.) Better take all I can carry. Who knows when i'll be able to get in here again!
End
